CGAL::Polyhedral_envelope.name: CGAL::Polyhedral_envelope
CGAL::Polyhedral_envelope.brief: This class can be used to check if a query point, segment, or triangle is inside or outside a polyhedral envelope of a set of triangles, constructed for a given $ \epsilon $ distance tolerance.
CGAL::Polyhedral_envelope.detailed: The polyhedral envelope is the union of prisms obtained. See Section Polyhedral Envelope Containment Check for more details. GeomTraits a geometric traits class, model of Kernel

members.name: Polyhedral_envelope
members.brief: Default constructor, envelope is empty

members.name: Polyhedral_envelope
members.brief: Constructor with a triangulated surface mesh.
members.detailed: TriangleMesh a model of VertexListGraph and FaceListGraph NamedParameters a sequence of Named Parameters tmesh a triangle mesh epsilon the distance of the Minkowski sum hull np an optional sequence of Named Parameters among the ones listed below Optional Named Parameters <button class="collapsible"> vertex_point_map </button> a property map associating points to the vertices of tmesh Type: a class model of ReadablePropertyMap with boost::graph_traits<PolygonMesh>::vertex_descriptor as key type and Point_3 as value type Default: boost::get(CGAL::vertex_point, tmesh) Extra: If this parameter is omitted, an internal property map for CGAL::vertex_point_t must be available in TriangleMesh . <button class="collapsible"> face_epsilon_map </button> a property map associating to each face of tm an epsilon value Type: a class model of ReadablePropertyMap with boost::graph_traits<TriangleMesh>::face_descriptor as key type and double as value type Default: Use epsilon for all faces The triangle mesh gets copied internally, that is it can be modified after having passed as argument, while the queries are performed

CGAL::Rigid_triangle_mesh_collision_dete.name: CGAL::Rigid_triangle_mesh_collision_detection
CGAL::Rigid_triangle_mesh_collision_dete.brief: This class provides methods to perform some intersection tests between triangle meshes that undergo affine transformations (rotation, translation, and scaling).
CGAL::Rigid_triangle_mesh_collision_dete.detailed: Meshes are added to an internal set and are referenced using an id assigned when added to the set. Note that the exact predicate framework applies on the meshes after having applied the transformation to the coordinates of the points of the vertices of each mesh. TriangleMesh a model of VertexListGraph , HalfedgeListGraph , and FaceListGraph VertexPointMap a model of ReadablePropertyMap with the vertex descriptor of TriangleMesh as key type, and a point from a CGAL kernel as value type. Default is the internal point property map of TriangleMesh , if it exists. Kernel a model of CGAL Kernel. Default is the kernel of the value type of VertexPointMap retrieved using Kernel_traits . AABBTree an AABB_tree that can containing faces of TriangleMesh . Default is using AABB_traits with AABB_face_graph_triangle_primitive as primitive type. Has_rotation tag indicating whether the transformations applied to meshes may contain rotations ( Tag_true ) or if only translations and scalings are applied ( Tag_false ). Some optimizations are switched on in case there are no rotations.

Type: Boolean Default: true the id of tm used to refer to that mesh.

members.brief: adds an instance of a triangulated surface mesh using an external tree of its faces.
members.detailed: The tree is not copied and the lifetime of tree must be longer than that of this class. NamedParameters a sequence of Named Parameters tree an AABB-tree of faces of a mesh tm triangulated surface mesh np an optional sequence of Named Parameters among the ones listed below Optional Named Parameters <button class="collapsible"> vertex_point_map </button> a property map associating points to the vertices of tm Type: a class model of ReadWritePropertyMap with boost::graph_traits<TriangleMesh>::vertex_descriptor as key type and Point_3 as value type Default: boost::get(CGAL::vertex_point, tm) Extra: If this parameter is omitted, an internal property map for CGAL::vertex_point_t should be available for the vertices of tm . <button class="collapsible"> face_index_map </button> a property map associating to each face of tm a unique index between 0 and num_faces(tm) - 1 Type: a class model of ReadablePropertyMap with boost::graph_traits<PolygonMesh>::face_descriptor as key type and std::size_t as value type Default: an automatically indexed internal map <button class="collapsible"> apply_per_connected_component </button> If false , tm is assumed to have only one connected component, avoiding the extraction of connected components Type: Boolean Default: true the id of tm used to refer to that mesh.

members.name: set_transformation
members.brief: sets the transformation associated to a mesh identified by its id in the set.

members.name: get_all_intersections
members.brief: returns a vector of the ids of meshes within ids that have at least a face intersecting a face of the mesh with id mesh_id .
members.detailed: If mesh_id is in ids , it is not reported. MeshIdRange a range of ids convertible to std::size_t .

members.name: get_all_intersections
members.brief: returns a vector of the ids of meshes in the set that have at least a face intersecting a face of the mesh with id mesh_id

members.name: get_all_intersections_and_inclusions
members.brief: returns a vector of the ids of meshes within ids that are intersecting with the mesh with id mesh_id , considering volume inclusions for closed meshes.
members.detailed: More precisely, if at least one face of a mesh with id i intersects a face of the mesh with id mesh_id , the pair (i, false) is put in the output vector. If there is no face intersection, but at least one of the meshes with ids i and mesh_id is closed, and at least one connected component is included in the bounded volume defined by a closed mesh then the pair (i, true) is put in the output vector (independently of mesh i or mesh_id being the one including the other). The inclusion test is done using Side_of_triangle_mesh , in particular surface orientation is ignored and only the nesting level of connected components defines a bounded volume. If a mesh has some self-intersection the inclusion test may return incorrect results. If mesh_id is in ids it is not reported. MeshIdRange a range of ids convertible to std::size_t . If a mesh is made of several connected components and at least one component is not closed, then no inclusion test will be made even if some components are closed.

members.name: get_all_intersections_and_inclusions
members.brief: returns a vector of the ids of meshes in the set that are intersecting with the mesh with id mesh_id , considering volume inclusions for closed meshes.
members.detailed: See the previous overload for details.

CGAL::Side_of_triangle_mesh.name: CGAL::Side_of_triangle_mesh
CGAL::Side_of_triangle_mesh.brief: This class provides an efficient point location functionality with respect to a domain bounded by one or several disjoint closed triangle meshes.
CGAL::Side_of_triangle_mesh.detailed: A point is said to be on the bounded side of the domain if an odd number of surfaces is crossed when walking from the point to infinity. The input triangle mesh is expected to contain no self-intersections and to be free from self-inclusions. In case the triangle mesh has several connected components, the same test is performed and returns correct results. In case of self-inclusions, the user should be aware that the predicate called inside every other sub-volume bounded by a nested surface will return in turns CGAL::ON_BOUNDED_SIDE and CGAL::ON_UNBOUNDED_SIDE , following the aforementioned parity criterion. This class depends on the package PkgAABBTree. TriangleMesh a triangulated surface mesh, model of FaceListGraph GeomTraits a geometric traits class, model of Kernel VertexPointMap a model of ReadablePropertyMap with boost::graph_traits<TriangleMesh>::vertex_descriptor as key type and GeomTraits::Point_3 as value type. The default is typename boost::property_map<TriangleMesh,vertex_point_t>::type . Implementation Details The current implementation is based on the number of triangles intersected by a ray having the query point as source. The do-intersect predicate used to detect if a triangle is intersected is able to detect if a triangle is intersected in its interior or on its boundary. In case it is intersected on its boundary, another ray is picked. In order to speed queries, the first ray used is an axis-aligned one that depends on the extents of the bbox of the input mesh. In case other rays are needed to conclude, the rays are generated from a random uniform sampling of a sphere.

PMPDistanceTraits.name: PMPDistanceTraits
PMPDistanceTraits.brief: The concept PMPDistanceTraits is a refinement of the concepts AABBGeomTraits_3 and SpatialSortingTraits_3 . In addition to the types required by those concepts, it also requires types and functors needed by the functions approximate_max_distance_to_point_set() , sample_triangle_mesh() , approximate_Hausdorff_distance() and max_distance_to_triangle_mesh() .
PMPDistanceTraits.detailed: Refines AABBGeomTraits_3 SpatialSortingTraits_3 Has models Any 3D Kernel is a model of this concept

members.brief: 3D point type It must be default constructible, and can be constructed from 3 objects of type FT .
members.detailed: bool operator<( Point_3 , Point_3 ) to lexicographically compare two points must be available. Access to Cartesian coordinates must be possible using Point_3::x() , Point_3::y(), Point_3::z() and FT operator[](int i) with 0 <= i < 3 . There must be a specialization of CGAL::Kernel_traits such that CGAL::Kernel_traits< Point_3 >::Kernel is a model implementing this concept.

PMPHolefillingVisitor.name: PMPHolefillingVisitor
PMPHolefillingVisitor.brief: The concept PMPHolefillingVisitor defines the requirements for the visitor used in hole-filling-related functions . The hole filling may use a 2D constrained triangulation for almost planar holes ( planar phase ). If that is not appropriate or fails it may use an algorithm with a quadratic running time relying on the 3D Delaunay triangulation ( quadratic phase ). If that fails, it uses an algorithm with cubic running time ( cubic phase ).

members.name: start_planar_phase
members.brief: called when the planar hole filling algorithm starts.

members.name: end_planar_phase
members.brief: called when the planar hole filling algorithm stops.
members.detailed: success true when the hole could be filled in this phase.

members.name: start_quadratic_phase
members.brief: called when the algorithm with quadratic running time starts.
members.detailed: n the upperbound on the number of steps

members.name: quadratic_step
members.brief: called at each step.
members.detailed: There may be less than n calls as this is an upperbound.

members.name: end_quadratic_phase
members.brief: called when the algorithm with quadratic running time ends.
members.detailed: success true when the hole could be filled in this phase.

members.name: start_cubic_phase
members.brief: called when the algorithm with cubic running time starts.
members.detailed: n the upperbound on the number of steps

members.name: cubic_step
members.brief: called at each step. This will be called n times.

members.name: end_cubic_phase
members.brief: called when the algorithm with cubic running time ends.

members.name: start_refine_phase
members.brief: called before refining the triangulation of the hole ( triangulate_and_refine_hole() and triangulate_refine_and_fair_hole() only)

members.name: end_refine_phase
members.brief: called after having refined the triangulation of the hole ( triangulate_and_refine_hole() and triangulate_refine_and_fair_hole() only)

members.name: start_fair_phase
members.brief: called before fairing the triangulation of the hole ( triangulate_refine_and_fair_hole() only)

members.name: end_fair_phase
members.brief: called after having faired the triangulation of the hole ( triangulate_refine_and_fair_hole() only)

members.name: accept_triangle
members.brief: called before considering a triangle made from the points with indices i0, i1, and i2 to triangulate a hole.
members.detailed: The indices refers to the position of the points in the input point range if triangulating a polyline, or to the position of the vertices along the cycle of halfedges of the input border halfedge if triangulating a hole in a polygon mesh ( 0 being the target of the input border halfedge, 1 the target of the next border halfedge, ...). If false is returned, the triangle will not be used. If true is returned, the triangle will be added to the candidate triangles.

PMPPolygonSoupOrientationVisitor.name: PMPPolygonSoupOrientationVisitor
PMPPolygonSoupOrientationVisitor.brief: The concept PMPPolygonSoupOrientationVisitor defines the requirements for the visitor used in CGAL::Polygon_mesh_processing::orient_polygon_soup() to track the detection of non-manifold simplices and the modifications performed to polygons during the orientation process.

members.name: non_manifold_edge
members.brief: called each time an edge appears in more than two polygons.
members.detailed: id1 and id2 are the vertex ids of the endpoints of the edge. nb_polygons indicates the number of polygons containing that edge.
members.section: Functions used to report non-manifold simplices.

members.name: non_manifold_vertex
members.brief: called each time a non-manifold vertex is detected.
members.detailed: vid is the id of the vertex that is non-manifold. nb_link_ccs is the number of edge connected components of vertices in the link of the corresponding vertex.
members.section: Functions used to report non-manifold simplices.

members.name: link_connected_polygons
members.brief: called during the detection of a non-manifold vertex, one time per incident edge connected component of vertices in the link of the vertex with id vid .
members.detailed: id is the id of the vertex that is non-manifold. polygon_ids contains the ids of the polygon in such a connected component. This function is called a number of times exactly equal to the parameter nb_link_ccs for the same vertex in non_manifold_vertex() . Note that the aforementioned function is called after all the calls to this function are done.
members.section: Functions used to report non-manifold simplices.

members.name: polygon_orientation_reversed
members.brief: called when the orientation of a polygon is not compatible with its neighbors and the polygon is reversed.
members.detailed: id is the index of the polygon in the input polygon range.
members.section: Functions used to report modifications done to the polygons.

members.name: duplicated_vertex
members.brief: called for each non-manifold vertex (whether part of a non-manifold edge or not).
members.detailed: Non-manifoldness is resolved in the algorithm by duplicating the vertex. input_id is the index of the input vertex, and new_id is the index of the new vertex. Note that a vertex might be duplicated several times.
members.section: Functions used to report modifications done to the polygons.

members.name: vertex_id_in_polygon_replaced
members.brief: called when the vertex with id input_id in polygon with id pid is replaced by the vertex with id new_id .
members.detailed: This function is called after all calls to duplicated_vertex() .
members.section: Functions used to report modifications done to the polygons.

PMPSelfIntersectionTraits.name: PMPSelfIntersectionTraits
PMPSelfIntersectionTraits.brief: Geometric traits concept for the functions CGAL::self_intersections() and CGAL::does_self_intersect() .

members.name: Point_3
members.brief: 3D point type

members.name: Triangle_3
members.brief: 3D triangle type

members.name: Segment_3
members.brief: 3D segment type

members.name: Construct_triangle_3
members.brief: Functor constructing triangles.
members.detailed: It provides Triangle_3 operator() const(const Point_3 &, const Point_3 &, const Point_3 &)

members.name: Construct_segment_3
members.brief: Functor constructing segments.
members.detailed: It provides Segment_3 operator() const(const Point_3 &, const Point_3 &)

members.name: Do_intersect_3
members.brief: Functor testing intersections between triangles and segments.
members.detailed: It provides bool operator() const (const Triangle_3 &, const Segment_3 &) and bool operator() const (const Triangle_3 &, const Triangle_3 &)

PMPSizingField.name: PMPSizingField
PMPSizingField.brief: The concept PMPSizingField defines the requirements for the sizing field used in CGAL::Polygon_mesh_processing::isotropic_remeshing() to define the target length for every individual edge during the remeshing process.

members.name: vertex_descriptor
members.brief: Vertex descriptor type.

members.name: halfedge_descriptor
members.brief: Halfedge descriptor type.

members.name: Point_3
members.brief: 3D point type matching the value type of the vertex property map passed to CGAL::Polygon_mesh_processing::isotropic_remeshing()

members.name: PolygonMesh
members.brief: Polygon mesh type matching the type passed to CGAL::Polygon_mesh_processing::isotropic_remeshing() .

members.name: FT
members.brief: Number type matching the FT type of the geometric traits passed to CGAL::Polygon_mesh_processing::isotropic_remeshing() .

members.name: at
members.brief: returns the sizing value at v (used during tangential relaxation).

members.name: is_too_long
members.brief: returns the ratio of the current edge squared length and the local target edge squared length between the points of va and vb in case the current edge is too long, and std::nullopt otherwise (used for triggering edge splits and preventing some edge collapses).
members.argsstring: (const vertex_descriptor va, const vertex_descriptor vb, const PolygonMesh &pmesh) const

members.name: is_too_short
members.brief: returns the ratio of the squared length of h and the local target edge squared length if it is too short, and std::nullopt otherwise (used for triggering edge collapses).

members.name: split_placement
members.brief: returns the position of the new vertex created when splitting the edge of h .

members.name: register_split_vertex
members.brief: function called after the addition of the split vertex v in pmesh .
members.detailed: This function can be used for example to update a pre-computed sizing field.

PMPTriangulateFaceVisitor.name: PMPTriangulateFaceVisitor
PMPTriangulateFaceVisitor.brief: The concept PMPTriangulateFaceVisitor defines the requirements for the visitor used in triangulation-related functions to track the creation of new faces.

members.name: before_subface_creations
members.brief: called before the triangulation of f_split .
members.detailed: Note that f_split will be one of the faces of the triangulation. Each subsequent call to after_subface_created() will correspond to the creation of a new face of the triangulation of f_split .
members.section: Functions used by triangulate_face() and triangulate_faces()

members.name: after_subface_creations
members.brief: called when the triangulation of a face in tm is finished.
members.section: Functions used by triangulate_face() and triangulate_faces()

members.name: after_subface_created
members.brief: called after creating a new triangle face f_new to triangulate the face passed to before_subface_creations() .
members.detailed: Note that the call is placed just after a call to add_face() so the halfedge pointer is not set yet.
members.section: Functions used by triangulate_face() and triangulate_faces()

members.name: accept_face
members.brief: called before considering a triangle made of vertices (v0,v1,v2) to triangulate f .
members.detailed: If false is returned, the triangle will not be used. If true is returned, the triangle will be added to the candidate triangles.
members.argsstring: (face_descriptor f, vertex_descriptor v0, vertex_descriptor v1, vertex_descriptor v2)
members.section: Functions used by triangulate_face() and triangulate_faces()

members.name: face_descriptor
members.brief: Face descriptor type.

members.name: vertex_descriptor
members.brief: Vertex descriptor type.

members.name: keep_or_remove_connected_components
members.argsstring: (PolygonMesh &pmesh, const ComponentRange &components_to_keep, const FaceComponentMap &fcm, bool keep, const NamedParameters &np=parameters::default_values())

members.name: sample_triangles
members.argsstring: (const FaceRange &triangles, const TriangleMesh &tm, VertexPointMap vpm, double distance, PointOutputIterator out, bool sample_faces, bool sample_edges, bool add_vertices)

members.name: bounded_error_Hausdorff_distance_naive
members.argsstring: (const TriangleMesh1 &tm1, const TriangleMesh2 &tm2, const double error_bound, const NamedParameters1 &np1=parameters::default_values(), const NamedParameters2 &np2=parameters::default_values())

members.name: barycentric_coordinates
members.argsstring: (const Point &p, const Point &q, const Point &r, const Point &query)

members.name: edge_length
members.argsstring: (typename boost::graph_traits< PolygonMesh >::edge_descriptor e, const PolygonMesh &pmesh, const NamedParameters &np=parameters::default_values())

members.name: squared_edge_length
members.argsstring: (typename boost::graph_traits< PolygonMesh >::edge_descriptor e, const PolygonMesh &pmesh, const NamedParameters &np=parameters::default_values())
